When Judas took the bread, Satan entered him. Jesus said to Judas, What you will do, do it quickly!
The other disciples did not understand why Jesus said to Judas that he must hurry.
Judas always had the money bag and some of the disciples thought that Jesus had told Judas to go and buy something for the feast or to give something to the poor people.
After Judas had taken the bread, he went out immediately. It was night.
After Judas had gone out, Jesus said: Now God will show that I, the Son of man, am also God. God has used Me to show that He is God.
Yes, God used Me to show that He is God and God will show that I am also God. He will not wait long. He will do it now.
My dear friends, I will be with you for only a short while. You will look for Me, but I tell you now what I have told the Jews: I am going to a place, but you can not come there.
I give you a new law now: You must love each other as I have loved you.
If you love each other, then everyone will know that you are my disciples.’
Simon-Peter said to Jesus: Lord, where are You going? Jesus said to him: You can not come with Me now. You will come later.
Peter said to Him: Lord, why can I not go with You now? I will die to protect You.
Jesus said to him: No, Peter, you said you will die to protect Me, but I tell you this and it is sure: Before the cock crows tomorrow morning, you will say 3 times that you do not know Me.

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Don't let your hearts be worried. You believe in God; believe in me also.
There are many rooms in my Father's house. If it were not true, I would have told you. I am going there to prepare a place for you.
And after I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you to be with me, so that you can be where I am.
You know the way to the place where I am going.
Thomas said to him: Lord, we don't know where you are going, so how can we know the way?
Jesus said to him: I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
If you really knew me, you would know my Father too. From now on you do know him, and you have seen him.
Philip said to him: Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.
Jesus said to him: Philip, I have been with you such a long time. Don't you know me yet? Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father. So how can you say, Show us the Father?
Don't you believe that I am in the Father, and the Father is in me? The words I say to you do not come from me alone. The Father, who lives in me, is doing his work through me.
Believe me when I say that I am in the Father, and the Father is in me. Or at least believe because of the works themselves.
I tell you the truth: anyone who believes in me will do the same works I do. He will do even greater works than these, because I am going to the Father.
And I will do anything you ask in my name, so that the Father will be honored through the Son.
If you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
If you love me, you will obey my commands.
And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper to be with you forever.
He is the Spirit of truth. The world cannot accept him, because it does not see him or know him. But you know him, because he lives with you and will be in you.
I will not leave you all alone. I will come to you.
Soon the world will not see me anymore, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you are in me, and I am in you.
Whoever has my commands and obeys them is the one who loves me. My Father will love the one who loves me, and I will love him and show myself to him.
Then Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said: Lord, why will you show yourself to us, and not to the world?
Jesus answered: If anyone loves me, he will obey my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
Anyone who does not love me does not obey my words. And these words you hear are not my own; they come from the Father who sent me.
I have told you all this while I am still with you.
But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things. He will remind you of everything I have told you.
I leave you peace. My own peace I give to you. I do not give to you the way the world gives. So don't let your hearts be worried, and don't be afraid.
You heard me say: I am going away, and I will come back to you. If you loved me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I am.
I have told you now, before it happens, so that when it does happen, you will believe.
I will not talk with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me.
But the world must know that I love the Father and that I do exactly what the Father has commanded me. Come now, let us go.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
He cuts off every branch in me that does not give fruit. And he trims every branch that does give fruit, so that it will give even more.
You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.
Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. A branch cannot give fruit by itself; it must stay joined to the vine. In the same way, you cannot give fruit unless you stay joined to me.
I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever stays joined to me, and I to him, will give much fruit. Without me you can do nothing.
Whoever does not stay joined to me is thrown away like a branch and dries up. Such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
If you stay joined to me, and my words stay in you, you may ask for anything you want, and it will be done for you.
This is how my Father is honored: that you give much fruit and show that you are my disciples.
I have loved you just as the Father has loved me. Now stay in my love.
If you obey my commands, you will stay in my love, just as I have obeyed my Father's commands and stay in his love.
I have told you this so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
This is my command: love one another, just as I have loved you.
No one has greater love than this: to give up his life for his friends.
You are my friends if you do what I command you.
I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know what his master is doing. Instead, I have called you friends, because I have told you everything I heard from my Father.
You did not choose me; I chose you. And I gave you this work: to go and give fruit that will last. Then the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
This is my command to you: love one another.
If the world hates you, remember that it hated me first.
If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. But you do not belong to the world. I have chosen you out of the world, so the world hates you.
Remember what I told you: a servant is not greater than his master. If people treated me badly, they will treat you badly too. If they obeyed my teaching, they will obey yours too.
They will do all this to you because of my name, because they do not know the One who sent me.
If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
Whoever hates me also hates my Father.
If I had not done among them the works that no one else has done, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have seen these works, and still they have hated both me and my Father.
But this happened so that the word written in their law would come true: They hated me for no reason.
When the Helper comes, I will send him to you from the Father. He is the Spirit of truth, who comes from the Father, and he will tell people about me.
And you also must tell people about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I have told you all this so that you will not give up your faith.
They will put you out of the synagogues. In fact, the time is coming when anyone who kills you will think he is serving God.
They will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
But I have told you, so that when the time comes, you will remember I warned you. I did not tell you this at the beginning, because I was with you.
Now I am going to the One who sent me, and none of you asks me: Where are you going?
Instead, your hearts are full of sadness because I have told you these things.
But I tell you the truth: it is better for you that I go away. If I do not go, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
When he comes, he will show the world the truth about sin, about being right with God, and about judgment.
He will show them about sin, because they do not believe in me.
He will show them about being right with God, because I am going to the Father, where you will not see me anymore.
And he will show them about judgment, because the ruler of this world has already been judged.
I still have much more to tell you, but it is too much for you now.
But when the Spirit of truth comes, he will lead you into all truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is coming.
He will honor me, because he will take what is mine and make it known to you.
Everything the Father has is mine. That is why I said the Spirit will take what is mine and make it known to you.
In a little while you will not see me. Then a little while later you will see me.

Some of his disciples said to one another: What does he mean? He says, In a little while you will not see me, and then a little while later you will see me. And he says, Because I am going to the Father.
They kept asking: What does he mean by, A little while? We don't understand what he is saying.

Jesus knew they wanted to ask him about it, so he said to them: Are you asking each other what I meant when I said, In a little while you will not see me, and then a little while later you will see me?
I tell you the truth: you will cry and be sad, but the world will be glad. You will grieve, but your sadness will turn into joy.
When a woman is giving birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when her baby is born, she forgets the pain, because she is so happy that a child has come into the world.
It is the same with you. Now you are sad, but I will see you again. Then your hearts will be glad, and no one can take away your joy.
On that day you will not need to ask me anything. I tell you the truth: my Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask, and you will receive, so that your joy will be full.
I have been speaking to you in word-pictures. But the time is coming when I will not speak that way anymore. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
On that day you will ask in my name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father for you.
The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
I came from the Father into the world. Now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.
Then his disciples said: Now you are speaking plainly, not in word-pictures.
Now we can see that you know everything, and that you do not even need anyone to ask you questions. This makes us believe that you came from God.
Jesus answered: Do you believe now?
Listen, the time is coming, and is already here, when you will all be scattered to your own homes. You will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
I have told you these things so that you can have peace in me. In this world you will have trouble. But be brave! I have defeated the world.

17.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After Jesus said this, he looked up to heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come. Honor your Son, so that your Son may honor you.
You gave him power over all people, so that he could give eternal life to all those you have given him.
And this is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
I have honored you here on earth. I have finished the work you gave me to do.
And now, Father, honor me in your presence with the glory I had with you before the world began.
I have made your name known to the people you gave me out of the world. They were yours; you gave them to me, and they have obeyed your word.
Now they know that everything you gave me comes from you.
For I gave them the words you gave me, and they accepted them. They know for sure that I came from you, and they believe that you sent me.
I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those you have given me, because they are yours.
All that is mine is yours, and all that is yours is mine. And I am honored through them.

I will not be in the world much longer, but they are still in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them safe by the power of your name, the name you gave me, so that they may be one, just as we are one.

While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power of your name, the name you gave me. I watched over them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who was bound to be lost, so that the Scripture would come true.
Now I am coming to you. I say these things while I am still in the world, so that they may have my full joy in them.
I have given them your word, and the world has hated them, because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them safe from the evil one.
They do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
Make them holy by your truth; your word is truth.
Just as you sent me into the world, I have sent them into the world.
For their sake I make myself holy, so that they too may be made holy by the truth.
I am not praying only for them. I am also praying for all who will believe in me through their message,
so that they may all be one. Father, just as you are in me and I am in you, may they also be one in us, so that the world may believe that you sent me.
I have given them the glory you gave me, so that they may be one, just as we are one:
I in them and you in me. May they be brought to complete unity, so that the world may know that you sent me and have loved them, just as you have loved me.
Father, I want those you have given me to be with me where I am. I want them to see my glory, the glory you gave me because you loved me before the world began.
Righteous Father, the world does not know you, but I know you, and these followers know that you sent me.
I have made your name known to them, and I will keep making it known, so that the love you have for me may be in them, and that I myself may be in them.

18.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When Jesus had finished praying, he went out with his disciples across the Kidron Valley. There was a garden there, and he and his disciples went into it.
Judas, who was going to betray him, knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with his disciples.
So Judas came to the garden, leading a group of soldiers and some officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees. They were carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
Jesus knew everything that was going to happen to him. So he stepped forward and asked them: Who are you looking for?
They answered: Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus said: I am he. And Judas, who betrayed him, was standing there with them.
When Jesus said, I am he, they drew back and fell to the ground.
So he asked them again: Who are you looking for? And they said: Jesus of Nazareth.
Jesus answered: I told you that I am he. So if you are looking for me, let these others go.
This happened so that the words he had spoken would come true: I have not lost one of those you gave me.
Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.
But Jesus said to Peter: Put your sword away! Should I not drink the cup the Father has given me?
Then the soldiers, their captain, and the Jewish officers arrested Jesus. They tied him up
and led him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
Caiaphas was the one who had told the Jewish leaders that it would be better for one man to die for the people.
Simon Peter and another disciple were following Jesus. That other disciple was known to the high priest, so he went with Jesus into the high priest's courtyard.
But Peter waited outside at the door. Then the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, came back, spoke to the girl at the door, and brought Peter in.
The girl at the door said to Peter: Aren't you also one of this man's disciples? He said: I am not.
It was cold, so the servants and officers had made a charcoal fire. They were standing around it, warming themselves. And Peter stood there with them, warming himself too.
Meanwhile the high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
Jesus answered: I have spoken openly to the world. I always taught in the synagogues and at the temple, where all the Jewish people come together. I said nothing in secret.
Why are you asking me? Ask the people who heard me. They know what I said.
When Jesus said this, one of the officers standing nearby slapped him in the face and said: Is that how you answer the high priest?
Jesus answered: If I said something wrong, then say what was wrong. But if I spoke the truth, why did you hit me?
Then Annas sent him, still tied up, to Caiaphas the high priest.
As Simon Peter stood warming himself, they asked him: Aren't you also one of his disciples? He denied it and said: I am not.
One of the high priest's servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, said: Didn't I see you with him in the garden?
Again Peter denied it, and at that moment a rooster crowed.

Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governor's palace. It was early morning. The Jewish leaders did not go inside, because they did not want to make themselves unclean; they wanted to be able to eat the Passover.
So Pilate came outside to them and asked: What charge are you bringing against this man?
They answered: If he were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.
Pilate said to them: Take him yourselves and judge him by your own law. But the Jewish leaders said: We are not allowed to put anyone to death.
This happened so that the words of Jesus would come true, about the kind of death he was going to die.
Then Pilate went back inside the palace, called Jesus, and asked him: Are you the King of the Jews?
Jesus answered: Is that your own question, or did others tell you about me?
Pilate answered: Am I a Jew? It was your own people and the chief priests who handed you over to me. What have you done?
Jesus said: My kingdom does not belong to this world. If it did, my servants would fight to keep me from being handed over to the Jewish leaders. But my kingdom is not from here.

So Pilate said to him: Then you are a king? Jesus answered: You are right to say that I am a king. I was born for this, and I came into the world for this: to tell people the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.
Pilate said to him: What is truth? After he said this, he went out again to the Jewish leaders and told them: I find nothing wrong with this man.
But it is your custom for me to set one prisoner free for you at the Passover. So do you want me to set free the King of the Jews?
They shouted back: No, not this man! Give us Barabbas! Now Barabbas was a robber.
Pilate told the soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip.
The soldiers made a crown of thorn and they put it on Jesus’ head. Then they put a purple robe on Him.
The soldiers said, Hail, King of the Jews (which means Good health! Peace! Long life to you, King of the Jews]! And they slapped Him in the face.
Pilate went outside again. He said to the Jews I am bringing Jesus out to you. I want you to know that I find nothing I can charge him with.
Then Jesus came outside. The crown of thorns was still on his head and Jesus had on the purple robe. Pilate said to the Jews: Look at this Man now.

When the chief priests saw Jesus, they shouted to Pilate: Kill him on a cross! Kill him on a cross!”. Pilate told them: No, You take him and nail him to a cross yourselves. I find nothing I can charge him with
The Jews said to Pilate: We have a law that says he must die, because he said he is the Son of God.
When Pilate heard what the Jews said, he became even more afraid.
Pilate went into the judgement hall again and he said to Jesus: ‘You must tell me where You come from. But Jesus did not answer Pilate.
Then Pilate said to Jesus: Why don’t You talk to me? I have the power to make you free or to kill you on a cross.
Jesus said to Pilate: The only power you have over me is the power given to you by God. That is why the people who brought Me to you have done a bigger sin than you have done.

When Pilate heard what Jesus said, he tried to let Jesus go free, but the Jews shouted: Anyone who makes himself a king is against Caesar. So if you let Jesus go free, that means you are not Caesar’s friend.

When Pilate heard what the Jews said, he took Jesus outside. Then Pilate went to the chair where he always judged, and he sat on it. The name of the place was the Pavement. (The Hebrew name of that place was Gabbata).
It was the day before Passover. On that day the Jews always prepared everything for Passover. It was about noon when Pilate said to the Jews: Look, here is your King.

They shouted loudly Take him away! Take him away! Kill him on a cross! Pilate asked them: Must I crucify Jesus, your King?’ The chief priests said to Pilate: Jesus is not our King. We have only one king and that is Caesar.
So Pilate handed Jesus to the chief priest to be killed on a cross.
Jesus carried his cross and they went out of the city to a place with the name of The Skull. (The Hebrew name of that place is Golgotha).
There the soldiers nailed Jesus to the cross. They also nailed 2 other men to crosses with Jesus, one on his left hand side and one on his right hand side.
Pilate told them to write a sign and put it on the cross. The sign said, Jesus of Nazareth, the king of the jews.
The sign was written in Aramaic, in Latin, and in Greek. Many of the Jews read this sign, because the place where Jesus was nailed to the cross was near the city.
The Jewish chief priests went to Pilate and said to him: You must not write: Jesus, King of the Jews. You must write: Jesus said He is the King of the Jews.
Pilate told them: I wrote it and I will not change it.

After the soldiers nailed Jesus to the cross, they took his clothes. They divided his clothes into 4 parts, one part for each soldier. Jesus had a garment that was all one piece of cloth woven from top to bottom. It did not have a seam.

The soldiers said to each other: We must not tear this garment into smaller pieces. We must draw lots to see who will get it. This happened because it is written in the Old Testament: They divided my clothes, and each one took some of it. They drew lots to get my garment. This is what the soldiers did.
Jesus’ mother was standing near his cross. Her sister was also there and Mary, the wife of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
Jesus saw his mother standing there, and the disciple whom He loved very much, John. Jesus said to his mother: This disciple is now your son.
And Jesus said to the disciple John: This woman is now your mother. From that day John took Jesus’ mother to live in his home.
Jesus knew that He had done all the work on earth that God had sent Him to do. Jesus said: I am thirsty. He said this because these words are written in the Old Testament.
There was a jar full of vinegar wine. The soldiers put a sponge into the vinegar wine and they put it on a hyssop-branch and held it out in front of Jesus mouth.
When Jesus tasted the vinegar wine, He said: Now I have finished my work that I had to do. Jesus bowed his head forward and He died.

This day was Preparation day. The next day was a special Sabbath day. The Jewish leaders did not want the bodies to stay on the cross on the Sabbath day. So they asked Pilate to order that the legs of the men be broken. And they asked that the bodies be taken down from the crosses
So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men on the crosses beside Jesus.
When the soldiers came to Jesus, they saw that He was dead, so they did not break Jesus bones.
One of the soldiers took a spear and he pierced the side of Jesus’ body with it. Immediately blood and water came out of his body.
The man who saw this happen told this to the other people and every word that he said is the truth. He knows that he speaks the truth so that you may believe.
All this happened because the Old Testament said it would happen. It says: The people will not break Jesus bones.
At another place in the Old Testament it is written: They will see him. People will look at the one they stabbed.

Joseph, from the town of Arimathea, was a disciple of Jesus but he did not tell anyone because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. After Jesus had died, Joseph went to Pilate and asked him if he could take the body of Jesus. Pilate said he could take it. Then Joseph took Jesus’ body from the cross.
Nicodemus went with Joseph. He was the man who had come to Jesus before and talked to him at night. He brought about 100 pounds of spices—a mixture of myrrh and aloes.
Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus covered Jesus’ body with the myrrh and aloes and they put pieces of cloth around his body. (The Jews always did this before they buried someone).
In the place where Jesus was killed on the cross, there was a garden. In the garden there was a new tomb. No one had ever been buried there before.

Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus took Jesus’ body and they buried it in that tomb because it was near them and it was the day before the special Sabbath day when the Jews prepared everything for that day.
Early that Sunday morning Mary Magdalene went to the tomb where Jesus was buried. It was still dark but she saw that someone had rolled away the stone that had been in front of the tomb.

Mary Magdalene ran to Simon Peter and to another disciple, John, that Jesus loved very much. Mary told Peter and John: Someone has taken away the Lord’s body from the tomb and we do not know where they have put Him.
Peter and John went to the tomb.
Peter and John ran to the tomb but John ran faster than Peter and John got there first.
John bowed over and looked into the tomb. He saw the pieces of cloth that were lying there but he did not go into the tomb.
Then Simon Peter also came there. Peter went into the tomb and he saw the long pieces of cloth.
Peter also saw the cloth that had been around Jesus’ head. The cloth was folded and it was not in the same place as the other cloths.

Then John who came to the tomb first went into the tomb. John saw that Jesus’ body was not there and John believed. John 20: 9 At that time the disciples did not understand the Old Testament where it says that Jesus will live again.
Peter and John went back home.
Mary stood outside the tomb. crying. While she was crying, she bent down and looked inside the tomb.
Mary saw 2 angels. They had white clothes. They were sitting where Jesus’ body had been before. One angel sat where Jesus’ head had been and the other angel sat where his feet had been.
The angels asked Mary: Why are you crying? Mary said: They have taken away my Lord’s body and I do not know where they have put Jesus.
After Mary had said this, Mary looked back and she saw Jesus. Jesus was standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.

Jesus said to her: Why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Mary thought that it was the man who worked in the garden and Mary asked Him: Sir, have you taken away my Lord’s body? If you took Him, please tell me where you put my Lord so I can go and get Him. John 20: 16 Jesus said to her: Mary. Then Mary turned to Jesus and said: Rabboni. (Rabboni is a Hebrew word that means: My Lord).

Jesus said to her: Do not hold Me. I have to go to my Father in heaven. You must go back to my brothers, the disciples. Go and tell them that I am going to my Father and my God. He is also your Father and your God now.
Mary Magdalene told the disciples: I have seen the Lord. Mary told them what Jesus had said to her.

That Sunday evening, 1st day of the week, the disciples were together. All the doors were closed and locked because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. Suddenly, Jesus was standing there among them. He said: Peace be with you.

After Jesus had said this, Jesus showed the disciples his hands and the side of his body where the soldier had pierced Him with his spear. The disciples were very happy when they saw the Lord. John 21 Again Jesus said to them: I give you my peace. The Father has sent Me. Now I send you.
After Jesus had said this, Jesus breathed on them and said: I give you the Holy Spirit.
If you forgive the sins of anyone, their sins are forgiven. If there is anyone whose sins you don’t forgive, their sins are not forgiven.
Thomas, one of the 12 disciples, also had another name. His other name was Didymus. He was not with the other disciples when Jesus appeared to them.

Later the disciples told Thomas: We have seen the Lord. Thomas said to them: No, I do not believe it. I will believe only when I see the wounds in Jesus hands where the nails were and when I can put my finger into those wounds. I will believe only when I can put my hand into the wound on the side of Jesus’ body.
8 days later the disciples were together again and Thomas was with them. The doors were locked, but Jesus came and He stood in front of them. He said to them: I give my peace to you.
Then Jesus said to Thomas: Come, put your finger into my wounds and look at my hands. Come and put your hand into the wound on the side of my body. Do not doubt. You must believe.
Thomas said to Jesus: You are my jLord and my God.
Jesus said to Thomas: Thomas, you believe because you see me. Great blessings belong to the people who believe without seeing me
Jesus did a lot of other miracles that the disciples saw, but those miracles are not written in this book.
The miracles that you read about in this book are written so that you can believe in Jesus hrist, the Son of God. If you believe in Him, you will have eternal life because you belong to Him.
Later, Jesus appeared again to his disciples at the Lake of Tiberias. This is how it happened:
There were some of the disciples together. Simon Peter, Thomas (called Didymus), Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, the two sons of Zebedee, and two other followers.

Simon Peter said to the Disciples: I am going fish. Then the disciples with him said to him: We will go with you. Right then they got into the boat and they went fishing. They fished all night and caught no fish.
The next morning, very early, Jesus was standing on the beach but the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
Jesus said to them: Children, did you catch any fish? They said to Him: No

Jesus said to them: You must throw your fishing net out on the right-hand side of the boat, then you will catch fish. They threw the fishing net into the water on the right-hand side and there were so many fish in the net that they could not pull the net into the boat.

The disciple that Jesus loved very much said to Peter: That is the Lord. When Simon-Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (because he did not have on all his clothes). Peter jumped into the water and went to Jesus.
The other disciples came in the boat and they pulled the fishing net that was full of fish behind them. They were not far from the beach, about 200 cubits.
When the disciples in the boat came to the beach, they saw the burning coals of a fire and fish and bread on the coals.
Jesus said to them: Bring some of the fish that you have caught.
Simon-Peter climbed into the boat and he pulled the fishing net onto the beach. The net was full of big fish. There were 153 fish in the net, but the net did not tear.
Jesus said to them: Come and eat breakfast. The disciples did not ask g the fish.
This was the 3rd time that Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death.

When the disciples had finished eating breakfast, Jesus said to Simon-Peter: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me more than these others do (with total commitment and devotion?) Peter said: Yes, Lord, You know that I love You. [with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend] Jesus said to Peter: feed the littlest of my people.

Jesus asked him the 2nd time: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me with total commitment and devotion? Peter said to Him: ‘Yes, Lord, You know that I love You with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend. Jesus said to Peter: Then you must be a shepherd to my people.

Jesus asked Peter the 3rd time: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me with a deep, personal affection for Me, as for a close friend? Peter was sad because Jesus had asked him 3 times if he loved Him. He said to Jesus: Lord, You know everything. You know that I love you with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend. Jesus said to Peter: Then you must take care of my people.

Peter, I tell you this and it is sure: When you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands and someone else will dress you. Then they will take you where you do not want to go.
When Jesus said this, He told Peter how he, Peter, would die and how he would honour God. Then Jesus said to Peter: Come with Me.

Peter looked back and he saw that the disciple that Jesus loved very much was following them. It was the same disciple who had sat next to Jesus when they were eating and who had asked Jesus: Lord, who is the one who will betray You?
When Peter saw the disciple that Jesus loved very much, he said to Jesus: Lord, what will happen to him?
Jesus said to Peter: If I want him to live until I come, you don’t have to worry about that. I told you to follow Me.

The disciples thought that this disciple that Jesus loved very much would never die. But they were wrong. Jesus did not say that this disciple would not die. Jesus said to Peter: If I want him to live until I come, you don’t have to worry about that.
The disciple that Jesus loved very much was the disciple who told all of this and who wrote it all in this book. We know that he told the truth.

Jesus did a lot of other things, but you do not read about them all in this book. If people wanted to write all of those things, then I don’t think there would be enough place in the world for all those books.
